reasoning:
1. The question asks whether Reserve Balances on 2026-03-22 (reflecting the 2026-03-18 Wednesday FRED release) will be higher than on 2026-03-15 (the 2026-03-11 release).
2. The current value (3.073T) is very high relative to the linear trend line, having surged over the last 6 consecutive weeks. The FRED trend analysis gives a mere 27% baseline probability of exceeding this threshold based on the linear model due to mean-reversion.
3. Furthermore, mid-March is structurally a period where the Treasury General Account (TGA) tends to swell due to Corporate Tax Day (March 15) tax receipts and mid-month Treasury settlements, which consequently drain Reserve Balances.
4. With forces of both statistical mean-reversion following a strong multi-week run-up and fundamental mid-month liquidity drains (TGA build) working against an increase, a drop in Reserve Balances seems more probable than another increase.
